items.prompt: How does self-supervised learning differ from standard supervised learning during pretraining?
choices: It creates supervisory signals from unlabeled data via pretext tasks instead of manual task labels
It always requires more hand labels than supervised learning
It forbids any later fine-tuning on labeled data
It only works on tabular data, never images or text
items.explain: Section 11.7 contrasts SSL pretext supervision with labeled input–output pairs.

items.prompt: Pretext tasks are the final production task the model must ship with.
items.explain: Pretext tasks are proxies that learn representations later fine-tuned for downstream tasks.

items.prompt: Which is a common vision pretext task?
choices: Jigsaw reassembly, masked context prediction, or rotation prediction
Paying crowd workers for every pixel label before pretraining
Training only on perfect medical gold sets
items.explain: Section 11.7 lists jigsaw, context prediction, and rotation as vision pretext examples.

items.prompt: In Example 11.15, what happens after contrastive pretraining on unlabeled product photos?
items.rubric: Fine-tune a linear head / small labeled set for category recognition.
items.sample_answer: A small labeled set fine-tunes a linear head for category recognition.

items.prompt: Masked language modeling is an NLP pretext task that predicts hidden tokens from context.
items.explain: BERT-style MLM is a central NLP pretext task in Section 11.7.
